response.justification: Exceptionally strong alignment on seniority, current role, and AI-native engineering context—already at re:cinq as Staff Engineer building AI infrastructure and agentic systems. Demonstrates distributed systems mastery (millions RPS microservices at Dunnhumby) and strong TypeScript. Critical gap: Python is explicitly required but completely absent from resume; polyglot background suggests learnability but unproven in Python. Career pattern shows value delivery but relatively short tenures across roles.
strengths: Currently Staff Engineer at re:cinq working directly on AI infrastructure, MCP frameworks, and agentic systems—perfect context alignment
Proven distributed systems mastery: handled millions HTTP requests/second, designed microservices, async I/O, high-performance databases, and real-time systems
Strong TypeScript/Node.js with demonstrated architectural thinking across full stack—backend, infrastructure, and DevOps
concerns: Python is a must-have requirement but completely absent from skills and work history despite stated polyglot experience
No explicit mention of on-call incident response, monitoring, or observability experience despite role requirement for operational ownership
Pattern of relatively short tenures (15 months at RIDE, 2 years at Dunnhumby) raises questions about depth of strategic initiative execution

key_factors.factor: Current role at re:cinq on AI infrastructure matches required context exactly
Missing Python as must-have technical skill
Distributed systems and high-throughput architecture experience proven at scale
